covered_earnings_window.plain_language: Weekend run as of Saturday, June 13, 2026: confirmed U.S.-listed companies reporting before the next U.S. market open on Monday, June 15, 2026. No still-actionable after-close reporters remain before that open; Friday, June 12 after-close reports were already released.

market_context_summary: Friday risk appetite improved into the weekend, with the S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq higher and crude lower on Iran peace headlines, but the tape remains sensitive to rates, consumer sentiment, and next week's Fed meeting.

excluded_companies.reason: Friday after-close result already released before this weekend run
Calendar timing appeared estimated rather than company-confirmed for June 15 before open
Confirmed Monday after-close report, outside the strict next-open window
Confirmed Monday after-close report, outside the strict next-open window

ranked_table.earnings_timing: 2026-06-15 before market open; call 8:30 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Services revenue growth, adjusted EBITDA, FY27 ramp tone
ranked_table.key_risk_to_call: Revenue ramp pushed out; leverage/synergy caution
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Powerfleet has the cleanest setup in this thin window because recurring services growth, adjusted EBITDA expansion, and deleveraging give the market concrete metrics to reward if the FY27 subscriber-ramp story stays credible.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Public estimates cluster near breakeven EPS and roughly $113M-$115M revenue, with Q3 revenue of $113.5M and adjusted EBITDA of $25.7M as the comparison base.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Services revenue continues double-digit growth, adjusted EBITDA margin holds near the Q3 improvement, and management validates the South Africa contract ramp and deleveraging trajectory.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: A merely flat revenue quarter, investment spending, or softer timing on the large subscriber opportunity could make the FY27 ramp feel delayed.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: MarketChameleon snippet showed AIOT options pricing around a high-teens implied earnings move; short-interest evidence was mixed but not ignored. Alpaca IEX snapshot showed a $4.23 last trade on June 12 and a wide closing quote, underscoring liquidity risk.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Third-party previews showed estimates around breakeven to $0.02 EPS and about $113M-$115M revenue; limited revision detail was available.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: 2026-06-15 before market open; webcast 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Adjusted EBITDA path and restatement optics
ranked_table.key_risk_to_call: Low bar plus cost-cut relief or cannabis squeeze
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: The base case leans down because the market is likely to focus on whether adjusted EBITDA improvement and cash burn can offset restatement noise, margin pressure, and a still-lossmaking cannabis backdrop.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: Sources showed Q4 estimates near a $0.06 EPS loss and roughly $53M-$54M U.S.-reported revenue, while the prior quarter beat revenue expectations but missed EPS expectations.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Cost reductions, cash balance, MTL Cannabis progress, or less-bad guidance could trigger a relief move from a low absolute share price.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: Restatement optics, weak gross margin, cash burn, or failure to show a credible FY2027 positive adjusted EBITDA path could dominate.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: MarketChameleon snippet showed historical CGC earnings moves around low double digits, and MarketBeat short-interest data showed 5.61% of float short as of May 29, 2026, so squeeze risk is real but not extreme.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Seeking Alpha and Benzinga-style previews pointed to a loss expectation around $0.06 per share; Seeking Alpha listed no recent EPS revisions in its earnings view.

ranked_table.earnings_timing: 2026-06-15 before market open; call 9:00 a.m. ET
ranked_table.expected_driver: Q4 revenue growth and gross margin
ranked_table.key_risk_to_call: Tiny float, customer/order timing, freight costs
ranked_table.one_sentence_thesis: Jerash has a modest upside setup if Q4 confirms the guided revenue rebound and acceptable gross margin, but thin trading and customer concentration keep it out of higher-conviction territory.
ranked_table.market_expectation_summary: MarketBeat showed consensus EPS of -$0.01 and expected revenue of $36.20M for Q4, while prior Q3 revenue rose 18% to $41.8M and gross margin improved to 16.9%.
ranked_table.upside_case_summary: Q4 revenue growth near the guided 23%-26% range, stable 14%-16% gross margin, and constructive customer commentary could produce a relief bid.
ranked_table.downside_case_summary: A revenue or margin shortfall, shipping/logistics pressure, or weak outlook from major apparel customers could erase the narrow edge.
ranked_table.options_or_positioning_summary: Listed-options/implied-move data was not meaningfully available; MarketBeat showed very low short interest at 0.65% of float, and Alpaca IEX showed only 905 shares traded on June 12, highlighting execution and liquidity risk.
ranked_table.analyst_revision_summary: Thin analyst coverage; Q4 consensus centered on -$0.01 EPS and $36.20M revenue.

qualified_names.why: Absolute score above 30, reversal risk below 40, medium evidence, low disparity
Thin window and insufficient number of low-risk high-conviction setups

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Directional score qualifies by magnitude, but reversal risk is too high
Directional score below 30 and reversal risk above the allocation threshold

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

consensus_method_note.disparity_definition: 0-100 measure of disagreement across three independent passes; lower means tighter agreement.
consensus_method_note.strongest_names_disparity: AIOT and CGC had low directional disparity, but CGC's high reversal risk reduced confidence; JRSH had low numerical disparity but one pass stayed Neutral because of liquidity and evidence limits.
consensus_method_note.effect_on_confidence_and_allocation: Low disparity helped AIOT qualify for partial research allocation, while high reversal risk or sub-30 score excluded the other names.
consensus_method_note.method_change_from_lessons: None; existing timing-confirmation, key-metric-first, forward-look, and reversal-risk guardrails were applied.

disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
